title: glyceria conferta subsp. pseudodistans
intro: Glyceria conferta subsp. pseudodistans, commonly known as a type of grass, is a plant that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and acting as an expectorant. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including helping to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and may also exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs for enhanced effects.
